rulings.Case Title: CHRISTOPHER CASHION VS. GREGORY D. SCHREIER, AS AN INDIVIDUAL AND AS ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for December 11, 2024 line 1. PLAINTIFF CHRISTOPHER CASHION MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION is GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. As to issues 1 and 2 re: landlord's duty, the motion is DENIED. Moving party failed to identify the specific duty owed by the landlord, the plaintiff seeks adjudication of and failed to tie the general duties owed under the law to the facts of this case. As to issues 14, 15, 16, 17, and 19, the motion is granted. Moving party shifted their burden and opposing party failed to create a triable issue of fact. As to the remaining issues, the motion is denied as triable issues of fact exists as specified in the opposition; there is no affirmative defense of "lack of tender" in the answer. See issue 12. The Court further notes that Answer filed on October 10, 2022 does not contain a pages 4, i.e. the affirmative defenses go from 5th to 11th. Answer filed on February 16, 2023 by co-defendant Schreier Family Properties, LLC contains all pages and sequentially numbered affirmative defenses. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: JAMES BONG ENTERPRISE, INC. VS. MARY BONG ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Staying Proceedings
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for December 11, 2024 line 5. DEFENDANT MARY BONG, CHARLES CHUA, CHARLESTON CHUA, KINGSTON CHUA NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION FOR ORDER STAYING PROCEEDINGS is GRANTED. This action is stayed pending the resolution of the probate proceeding regarding James Bong Enterprise, Inc. No later than December 16, 2024 Defendants to post $4,075 with the Court. rulings.Case Title: SFII 1390 MARKET ST LLC VS. ALLONTE GAINO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: / Motion For Award Of Attorney'S Fee And Court Costs
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for December 11, 2024 line 6. PLAINTIFF SFII 1390 MARKET ST LLC / MOTION FOR AWARD OF ATTORNEY'S FEE AND COURT COSTS is DENIED. Moving party failed to provide any authority for the position that this motion is being timely made. The burden is on the moving party to establish the timeliness of the motion. Moving party failed to provide competent evidence of the existence of a contract with an attorney's fees clause, i.e. there is no declaration from a party to the lease or a custodian of record; there is no certified transcript of trial, where the lease was purportedly admitted into evidence. The Court further notes that per the Notice of Motion this motion is purportedly based, inter alia, on "the Declaration of Patricia Blair." There is no such declaration filed with the motion. There is a declaration of Anna Felton, which is not mentioned in the Notice of Motion. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: TENDERLOIN NEIGHBORHOOD DEVELOPMENT CORPORATION VS. BRANDON WATTS ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion Of Plaintiff To: 1) Compel Responses By Defendant To Form Interrogatories - Unlawful Detainer (Set One) Without Objections; 2) Deem Admitted By Defendant Requests For Admission (Set One), And For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for December 11, 2024 line 9. PLAINTIFF TENDERLOIN NEIGHBORHOOD DEVELOPMENT CORPORATION NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION OF PLAINTIFF TO: 1) COMPEL RESPONSES BY DEFENDANT TO FORM INTERROGATORIES - UNLAWFUL DETAINER (SET ONE) WITHOUT OBJECTIONS; 2) DEEM ADMITTED BY DEFENDANT REQUESTS FOR ADMISSION (SET ONE), AND FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS is GRATNED, subject to opposition. Admissions are deemed admitted. Defendant to provide complete verified responses without objections to From Interrogatories within 5 days of notice of entry of order. Request for sanctions is denied. The Court notes that Plaintiff used clearly recycled motion papers for which 2 hours of work at $500 are claimed. E.g. see proof of service referencing "Defendant Appollonia Jordan," not a party to this action and not the defendant allegedly served by overnight mail. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). rulings.Case Title: DELAWARE LAKEWOOD APARTMENTS, LLC VS. WENDY MENA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Deeming Admitted Plaintiff'S Requests For Admissions, Set One, To Defendant(S) And Request For Sanctions In The Amount Of $810 Against Defendat(S) Pursuant To California 2033.280(C); Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declaration In Support Thereof
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for December 11, 2024 line 10. PLAINTIFF DELAWARE LAKEWOOD APARTMENTS, LLC NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION FOR ORDER DEEMING ADMITTED PLAINTIFF'S REQUESTS FOR ADMISSIONS, SET ONE, TO DEFENDANT(S) AND REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS IN THE AMOUNT OF $810 AGAINST DEFENDAT(S) PURSUANT TO CALIFORNIA 2033.280(C); MEMORANDUM OF POINTS AND AUTHORITIES; DECLARATION IN SUPPORT THEREOF is DENIED. Moving party failed to establish that defendants did not timely comply with their obligations under the discovery act. Moving party claims that discovery was served via overnight mail on October 29, 2024. Moving party then declares under the penalty of perjury that based on this service the response were due, but have not been provided "by October 14, 2024," i.e. 15 days before the requests have been served. (Declaration of Leslie Salvador, paragraphs 3 and 4). =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849).
